chunk_summary: Guidelines for accurate MUAC, weight, height, and oedema assessment in outpatient care for MAM, emphasizing standardization, repeat measurements, and safety in borderline cases.
learning_objectives: Understand the correct technique for measuring MUAC, weight, and height in children with MAM.
Learn to assess bilateral pitting oedema systematically and differentiate it from local pathology.
Recognize the importance of repeating borderline measurements and documenting confirmed values.
Appreciate the role of standardized tools and techniques in reducing misclassification.
key_facts: MUAC should be measured on the left arm using a non-stretch tape, identifying the midpoint between the acromion and olecranon.
Measurements must be repeated at least twice and documented to the nearest millimeter.
Bilateral pitting oedema is confirmed by pressing both feet for several seconds and observing persistent pitting.
Small errors in measurement can shift a child across MAM thresholds, necessitating repeat measurements for borderline values.
In India, program entry criteria may include both MUAC and WHZ/WLZ, with local variations in definitions.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: World Health Organization. 2023. Guideline on the prevention and management of wasting and nutritional oedema in infants and children under 5 years.
